_meta.purpose: Meditation 系列 9 篇单源数据层（文章 + 未来 meditation 工具共用）
_meta.framework: 03-内容策略/内容Brief/模板-Meditation文章框架.md v2
_meta.compliance_baseline: support/invite/gentle companion/mindful anchor; 禁 cures/heals/treats anxiety/insomnia/depression
_meta.meaning_url_pattern: /gemstone/{meaning_slug}-meaning/ (只链已验证200的，未上线不强链)

focus.name: Focus & Concentration
focus.pain_point: fragmented attention, constant context-switching, mental fog
focus.hold_hand: right (traditionally the releasing side, letting distraction flow out)
focus.visualization: a single stable point of soft light, growing slightly brighter with each breath
eastern: Tibetan shamatha (single-pointed concentration meditation, calming the mind onto one object)
yoga trataka (candle or point gazing, a concentration practice)
focus.affirmation: My attention is steady and clear.
focus.compliance_phrasing: supporting attention / single-pointed practice (NOT cures ADHD / treats attention disorders)

sleep.name: Sleep & Bedtime Ritual
sleep.pain_point: racing mind at night, difficulty winding down, restless transition to rest
sleep.hold_hand: left (traditionally the receiving side, inviting settling)
sleep.scan_focus: crown of the head, then a slow release down the body
sleep.visualization: a curtain of dark soft velvet lowering slowly over the mind, dimming thought
eastern: Tibetan Dream Yoga / Milam (the tradition of using sleep and dream states as a field of practice)
Yoga Nidra (yogic sleep, the origin of the modern body-scan relaxation technique)
sleep.affirmation: I allow the day to settle, and rest to come.
sleep.compliance_phrasing: bedtime ritual / wind-down practice / settling into rest (NOT cures insomnia / treats sleep disorders)

emotional.name: Emotional Release
emotional.pain_point: held feelings, emotional weight, tension in the chest
emotional.breath: sighing breath (double inhale through nose, long slow exhale through mouth)
emotional.visualization: gentle water moving through and carrying accumulated feeling outward
eastern: Metta Bhavana (the Buddhist loving-kindness practice of cultivating warmth toward oneself and others)
heart-opening yoga tradition (Anahata, gentle chest-opening postures paired with breath)
emotional.affirmation: I make space for what I feel.
emotional.compliance_phrasing: emotional release practice / holding space for feelings (NOT treats depression / heals trauma)

grounding.name: Grounding & Stability
grounding.pain_point: feeling scattered, unrooted, overwhelmed, disconnected from the body
grounding.breath: earth breath (short inhale, long slow exhale as if pressing down into the ground)
grounding.hold_hand: both hands cupping the stone in the lap, feet flat on the floor
grounding.scan_focus: soles of the feet and base of the spine (root center)
grounding.visualization: roots growing from the soles of the feet down into cool, stable earth
eastern: yoga grounding asana tradition (Tadasana / mountain pose, Vrksasana / tree pose — postures of stable contact)
Daoist and jade-culture 'returning to the earth' — stones as a link to ground and origin
grounding.affirmation: I am steady, supported, and here.
grounding.compliance_phrasing: grounding touchstone / settling practice (NOT blocks negative energy / cures anxiety)

beginners.name: Beginners
beginners.pain_point: not knowing where to start, overthinking the process
beginners.breath: natural breath, silently counting ten cycles
beginners.hold_hand: left, holding one stone in the palm
beginners.scan_focus: simple path from palm to heart
beginners.visualization: a small warm sphere of soft light
eastern: the opening limb of yoga tradition (the early limbs of ashtanga that prepare the mind for meditation)
Japanese Zen zazen (the simple seated tradition, minimal instruction, just sit)
beginners.compliance_phrasing: a simple starting practice / gentle introduction

manifestation.name: Manifestation & Intention
manifestation.pain_point: vague goals, scattered will, difficulty holding a clear aim
manifestation.breath: rising breath (inhale 4, hold gently at the top 2, exhale 6)
manifestation.visualization: a scene in which a chosen aim has already taken shape, felt in the body rather than watched
eastern: the Tibetan kriya tradition (body, speech, and mind aligned in action toward an aim)
yoga sankalpa (the practice of setting a heartfelt intention or resolve at the start of practice)
manifestation.affirmation: I align body, breath, and aim.
manifestation.compliance_phrasing: tactile cue for an intention / aligning practice (NOT attracts wealth / guarantees success)

amethyst.name: Amethyst
amethyst.mineral: SiO₂ (quartz variety, color from natural irradiation of iron)
amethyst.science_angle: purple quartz, Mohs 7, color-sensitive to prolonged direct sun; cool smooth surface as a tactile anchor
amethyst.tradition_angle: long associated with clarity and stillness; crown and third-eye placement in many traditions
amethyst.mindfulness_angle: holding a cool stone during breathwork gives the mind a single point of contact
amethyst.safety: avoid prolonged direct sunlight (color fade); moonlight/selenite/sound safe

clear-quartz.name: Clear Quartz
clear-quartz.science_angle: Mohs 7, piezoelectric (used in watches and electronics); transparent clarity as a neutral visual object
clear-quartz.tradition_angle: described across cultures as a 'master' or all-purpose stone; placed anywhere on the body
clear-quartz.mindfulness_angle: a neutral, colorless focal point that doesn't compete with visualization imagery
clear-quartz.safety: durable, tolerates brief indirect sun; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

fluorite.name: Fluorite
fluorite.science_angle: Mohs 4 (relatively soft), famous for octahedral cleavage and layered color bands; a visual organizer of stripes
fluorite.tradition_angle: associated in modern crystal traditions with order and mental structure
fluorite.mindfulness_angle: the structured color bands become a visual metaphor for sorting scattered thoughts
fluorite.safety: soft and slightly water-sensitive; keep dry, moonlight/selenite/sound safe

rose-quartz.name: Rose Quartz
rose-quartz.mineral: SiO₂ (quartz variety, pink from titanium/manganese + irradiation)
rose-quartz.science_angle: Mohs 7, color fades with prolonged direct sun; soft pink as a calming visual tone
rose-quartz.tradition_angle: associated across cultures with gentleness and the heart center
rose-quartz.mindfulness_angle: a smooth palm stone that fits the curve of the hand, a natural 'worry stone' shape
rose-quartz.safety: avoid prolonged direct sun; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

citrine.name: Citrine
citrine.mineral: SiO₂ (quartz variety, color from iron + heat)
citrine.science_angle: Mohs 7, durable and relatively color-stable; warm yellow-orange tone
citrine.tradition_angle: historically associated with warmth, sunlight, and abundance imagery
citrine.mindfulness_angle: a warm-toned tactile cue for a positive intention or resolve
citrine.safety: durable, tolerates short indirect sun; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

selenite.name: Selenite
selenite.science_angle: Mohs 2 (very soft), fibrous and light-transmitting; can be scratched or degraded by prolonged water
selenite.tradition_angle: described in many traditions as self-cleansing; used as a plate to rest other stones on
selenite.mindfulness_angle: a long wand or flat plate that becomes a 'resting place' between sessions
selenite.safety: keep dry (avoid water/salt); moonlight/sound safe — the safest universal option

black-tourmaline.name: Black Tourmaline (Schorl)
black-tourmaline.science_angle: Mohs 7-7.5, pyroelectric (generates charge with temperature change); dense and heavy for its size
black-tourmaline.tradition_angle: a popular grounding and protective stone across Western and Eastern traditions
black-tourmaline.mindfulness_angle: the noticeable weight in the hand is a strong tactile anchor for grounding practice
black-tourmaline.safety: durable and sun-stable; brief water safe; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

smoky-quartz.name: Smoky Quartz
smoky-quartz.mineral: SiO₂ (quartz variety, color from natural irradiation)
smoky-quartz.science_angle: Mohs 7, color is natural irradiation (stable); translucent brown-grey tone
smoky-quartz.tradition_angle: associated with grounding and release in many modern crystal traditions
smoky-quartz.mindfulness_angle: a translucent stone that pairs visual softness with grounding weight

hematite.name: Hematite
hematite.science_angle: Mohs 5.5-6.5, distinctly heavy (high specific gravity), metallic grey-black; noticeably dense in the hand
hematite.tradition_angle: associated across cultures with grounding and stability, linked to iron and the earth
hematite.mindfulness_angle: the unusual weight immediately draws attention downward — a natural grounding cue
hematite.safety: keep dry (may rust in prolonged water); moonlight/selenite/sound safe

howlite.name: Howlite
howlite.science_angle: Mohs 3.5 (soft), naturally white with grey veining; porous so often dyed in jewelry
howlite.tradition_angle: associated in modern traditions with calm and a quiet mind at bedtime
howlite.mindfulness_angle: a soft pale stone often tucked under the pillow as part of a wind-down ritual
howlite.safety: soft and porous; keep dry; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

moonstone.name: Moonstone
moonstone.science_angle: Mohs 6-6.5, displays adularescence (a floating light sheen from layered feldspar structure)
moonstone.tradition_angle: associated across many cultures with lunar cycles, intuition, and new beginnings
moonstone.mindfulness_angle: the moving sheen gives the eye a soft, non-fixed point of rest
moonstone.safety: relatively soft; brief water safe; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

lepidolite.name: Lepidolite
lepidolite.science_angle: Mohs 2.5-3 (very soft), contains natural lithium; flaky scaly structure, lilac to grey-purple
lepidolite.tradition_angle: associated in modern traditions with a calm, settled state
lepidolite.mindfulness_angle: a soft cool stone often held during a wind-down or settling practice
lepidolite.safety: soft and layered; keep dry; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

labradorite.name: Labradorite
labradorite.science_angle: Mohs 6-6.5, displays labradorescence (flashes of blue/gold from internal layering)
labradorite.tradition_angle: associated with intuition and inner sight in many traditions
labradorite.mindfulness_angle: the unexpected flashes give the mind a gentle point of surprise to settle onto
labradorite.safety: durable; brief water safe; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

pyrite.name: Pyrite
pyrite.science_angle: Mohs 6-6.5, metallic gold, forms cubic crystals; can produce sparks (the origin of its name, 'fire stone')
pyrite.tradition_angle: historically associated with the sun, vitality, and confident action
pyrite.mindfulness_angle: the bright reflective surface as a visual cue for a confident, sun-like intention

rhodochrosite.name: Rhodochrosite
rhodochrosite.science_angle: Mohs 3.5-4 (soft), pink to rose-red with white banding; a manganese carbonate
rhodochrosite.tradition_angle: associated in modern traditions with the heart and self-compassion
rhodochrosite.mindfulness_angle: the soft pink banding as a gentle focal point for a heart-centered practice
rhodochrosite.safety: soft and sensitive to acids/water; keep dry; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

kyanite.name: Kyanite
kyanite.science_angle: Mohs 4.5-7 (variable by direction, anisotropic hardness), bladed crystal form, blue
kyanite.tradition_angle: described in some traditions as not needing cleansing; associated with alignment
kyanite.mindfulness_angle: the blade-like form and blue tone as an image of 'lining things up'
kyanite.safety: variable hardness, slightly brittle; keep dry; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

jade.name: Jade
jade.science_angle: Mohs 6-7 (nephrite) / 7 (jadeite), exceptionally tough (interlocking microcrystals), smooth cool surface
jade.tradition_angle: central to Chinese culture for millennia — a stone of integrity, calm, and cultivated virtue
jade.mindfulness_angle: the exceptional smoothness and cool-then-warming feel is one of the most pleasant tactile anchors

tiger-eye.name: Tiger's Eye
tiger-eye.science_angle: Mohs 7, chatoyancy (a moving 'cat's eye' line from parallel fibrous inclusions)
tiger-eye.tradition_angle: associated with steady alertness and grounded confidence
tiger-eye.mindfulness_angle: the moving eye-line gives the gaze a stable, traveling point of focus
tiger-eye.safety: durable; brief water safe; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

carnelian.name: Carnelian
carnelian.science_angle: Mohs 6.5-7, translucent orange-red from iron oxide; smooth waxy luster
carnelian.tradition_angle: associated across ancient cultures (Egypt, Rome) with vitality and courageous action
carnelian.mindfulness_angle: warm translucent tone as a cue for gentle activation rather than agitation
carnelian.safety: durable; brief water safe; moonlight/selenite/sound safe

articles.title: Best Crystals for Meditation: 13 Stones by Goal (Focus, Sleep, Calm)
articles.concept_focus: 13 crystals overview organized by meditation goal + scene routing matrix to M1-M8
eastern: yoga nyasa (placing stones along the body's centers)
Tibetan singing bowl as opening/closing sound

howto_6_steps.name: Settle with a few grounding breaths
howto_6_steps.text: begin with slow natural breaths to arrive in the space

howto_6_steps.name: Choose a stone that matches your goal today
howto_6_steps.text: let the intention (focus/sleep/calm/etc.) guide the choice, not rarity

howto_6_steps.name: Hold or place the stone
howto_6_steps.text: in the palm, on the lap, or on the body center that matches the goal

howto_6_steps.name: Rest your attention on the stone's texture
howto_6_steps.text: weight, temperature, surface — a single tactile point

howto_6_steps.name: Set a one-line intention
howto_6_steps.text: a simple phrase for this session

howto_6_steps.name: Close by thanking the practice
howto_6_steps.text: place the stone down gently and transition back

articles.h1: How to Use Crystals for Focus Meditation
articles.concept_focus: focus scene 6-step script (box breathing, third-eye, single-pointed) + fluorite/clear-quartz/hematite
eastern: Tibetan shamatha (single-pointed concentration)
yoga trataka (steady gazing)

howto_6_steps.name: Settle with box breathing
howto_6_steps.text: inhale 4, hold 4, exhale 4, hold 4 — repeat four rounds to steady the mind

howto_6_steps.name: Hold the stone in the right hand
howto_6_steps.text: traditionally the releasing side; let scattered attention flow outward with each exhale

howto_6_steps.name: Bring attention to the third-eye point
howto_6_steps.text: between the brows, resting the stone there if lying back or holding it near

howto_6_steps.name: Visualize a single stable point of light
howto_6_steps.text: a small soft point that grows slightly brighter with each breath

howto_6_steps.name: Set a one-line intention
howto_6_steps.text: "My attention is steady and clear."

howto_6_steps.name: Close by opening the eyes onto one object
howto_6_steps.text: rest the gaze on a single physical object for 30 seconds before moving on

articles.title: Crystals for Sleep Meditation: Bedtime Ritual Stones & Script
articles.h1: How to Use Crystals for Sleep Meditation
articles.concept_focus: sleep bedtime ritual 6-step script (4-7-8 breath, crown, velvet curtain) + amethyst/howlite/selenite/moonstone/lepidolite
eastern: Tibetan Dream Yoga / Milam (sleep as a field of practice)
Yoga Nidra (yogic sleep, the origin of body-scan relaxation)

howto_6_steps.name: Settle with 4-7-8 breath
howto_6_steps.text: inhale through the nose for 4, hold gently for 7, exhale through the mouth for 8 — repeat four cycles

howto_6_steps.name: Hold the stone in the left hand
howto_6_steps.text: traditionally the receiving side; or place it on the nightstand, or under the pillow

howto_6_steps.name: Scan from the crown downward
howto_6_steps.text: begin at the top of the head and let each slow exhale release the body one layer at a time

howto_6_steps.name: Visualize a curtain of dark velvet lowering
howto_6_steps.text: a soft dark curtain slowly dimming the mind, thought by thought

howto_6_steps.name: Set a one-line intention
howto_6_steps.text: "I allow the day to settle, and rest to come."

howto_6_steps.name: Close by setting the stone aside
howto_6_steps.text: place it on the nightstand or under the pillow, turn onto your side, and let the practice dissolve into sleep

articles.title: Crystals for Emotional Release Meditation: Stones & Healing Practice
articles.h1: How to Use Crystals for Emotional Release Meditation
articles.concept_focus: emotional release 6-step script (sighing breath, heart center, flowing water) + rose-quartz/rhodochrosite/smoky-quartz/amethyst/citrine
eastern: Metta Bhavana (Buddhist loving-kindness cultivation)
heart-opening yoga tradition (Anahata)

howto_6_steps.name: Settle with a sighing breath
howto_6_steps.text: a double inhale through the nose, followed by a long slow exhale through the mouth — repeat three times

howto_6_steps.name: Hold the stone in the left hand over the heart
howto_6_steps.text: rest the hand and stone on the center of the chest, over the sternum

howto_6_steps.name: Bring attention to the heart center
howto_6_steps.text: feel the weight of the stone and the gentle movement of breath beneath it

howto_6_steps.name: Visualize moving water
howto_6_steps.text: imagine a gentle current moving through the chest, carrying accumulated feeling outward with each exhale

howto_6_steps.name: Set a one-line intention
howto_6_steps.text: "I make space for what I feel."

howto_6_steps.name: Close with three things
howto_6_steps.text: before lifting the hand, silently name three small things you are grateful for in this moment

articles.concept_focus: grounding 6-step script (earth breath, root center, roots into soil) + black-tourmaline/hematite/smoky-quartz/jade/red-jasper-substitute
eastern: yoga grounding asana (Tadasana/Vrksasana tradition)
Daoist and jade-culture 'returning to the earth'

howto_6_steps.name: Settle with the earth breath
howto_6_steps.text: a short gentle inhale, a long slow exhale as if pressing weight down into the floor — repeat six rounds

howto_6_steps.name: Cup the stone in both hands in the lap
howto_6_steps.text: feet flat on the floor, hands cradling the stone, feel its weight

howto_6_steps.name: Bring attention to the soles of the feet
howto_6_steps.text: the contact with the floor, the base of the spine, the root center

howto_6_steps.name: Visualize roots growing down
howto_6_steps.text: from the soles of the feet, roots extending down into cool, stable, dark earth

howto_6_steps.name: Set a one-line intention
howto_6_steps.text: "I am steady, supported, and here."

howto_6_steps.name: Close by feeling the floor
howto_6_steps.text: before opening the eyes, press the feet gently into the floor and feel the support for a few breaths

articles.title: Meditation Crystals for Beginners: 5-Step Start + 6 Stones

eastern: the opening limbs of yoga tradition
Japanese Zen zazen (just sit)

howto_6_steps.name: Find a quiet seat and breathe naturally
howto_6_steps.text: no special technique — just notice ten natural breaths, silently counting

howto_6_steps.name: Hold one stone in the left palm
howto_6_steps.text: choose one stone only; feel its weight, temperature, and surface

howto_6_steps.name: Rest your attention on the stone
howto_6_steps.text: when the mind wanders, gently return to the feeling of the stone — that is the whole practice

howto_6_steps.name: Picture a small warm sphere of light
howto_6_steps.text: a simple image, nothing elaborate, resting in the palm with the stone

howto_6_steps.name: Set a simple intention
howto_6_steps.text: "I begin, simply." — one short line is enough

howto_6_steps.name: Close with a small smile
howto_6_steps.text: lift the corners of the mouth gently, open the eyes slowly, set the stone down

articles.h1: How to Do a Manifestation Meditation with Crystals
articles.concept_focus: manifestation 6-step script (rising breath, solar plexus, scene-already-realized) + citrine/pyrite/clear-quartz/carnelian

yoga sankalpa (heartfelt resolve)

howto_6_steps.name: Settle with the rising breath

howto_6_steps.name: Hold the stone in the right hand over the solar plexus
howto_6_steps.text: rest the hand just above the navel; feel the warmth of the hand and stone

howto_6_steps.name: Bring attention to the solar plexus

howto_6_steps.name: Set the resolve

howto_6_steps.name: Close by touching the stone
howto_6_steps.text: press the stone gently three times as a tactile seal, then set it down and carry one small next action into the day

articles.title: Crystal Meditation Script: 5 Ready-to-Use Guided Sessions
articles.h1: 5 Crystal Meditation Scripts You Can Use Today
articles.concept_focus: 5 ready-to-read scripts (focus/sleep/emotional/grounding/manifestation) — each a condensed 6-step from M1-M6, formatted to be read aloud or silently
eastern: integrates Tibetan shamatha, Dream Yoga, Metta, grounding asana, and yoga sankalpa across the five scripts

howto_6_steps.name: Pick one script and one stone
howto_6_steps.text: match the script to what you need today

howto_6_steps.name: Find a quiet seat or lie down
howto_6_steps.text: 5-15 minutes, undisturbed

howto_6_steps.name: Read the script once silently
howto_6_steps.text: to feel the shape of it

howto_6_steps.name: Hold the stone and follow the steps
howto_6_steps.text: at your own pace, breath by breath

howto_6_steps.name: Close as the script describes
howto_6_steps.text: each script has its own closing

howto_6_steps.name: Cleanse the stone before next use
howto_6_steps.text: see the cleansing timer for the safe method for your stone

articles.title: Meditation Room Crystals: How to Set Up Your Space
articles.h1: How to Set Up Crystals in Your Meditation Room
secondary_kw: how to arrange crystals in meditation room
articles.concept_focus: space setup (placement grid, the 4 corners + center layout, light/sound/scent layering) + selenite/clear-quartz/amethyst/black-tourmaline
eastern: Tibetan shrine / altar arrangement tradition (purifying and ordering a space)
Japanese Zen 'chashitsu' aesthetic of simple uncluttered space

howto_6_steps.name: Choose a quiet, low-traffic corner
howto_6_steps.text: a place you return to, even a small one — consistency matters more than size

howto_6_steps.name: Clean the space physically first
howto_6_steps.text: a clean surface and aired room — the material foundation

howto_6_steps.name: Place four stones at the corners
howto_6_steps.text: traditionally dark or grounding stones at the corners for a sense of boundary

howto_6_steps.name: Place one central stone
howto_6_steps.text: a clear quartz or selenite in the center as the focal point

howto_6_steps.name: Add one sensory layer (light, sound, or scent)
howto_6_steps.text: a candle, a singing bowl, or incense — one only, to keep it simple

howto_6_steps.name: Cleanse the stones before first use
howto_6_steps.text: moonlight or a selenite plate overnight — then the space is ready

intent: Which crystals are best for a meditation room?
How do I arrange crystals in my meditation space?
Where should I place selenite in a meditation room?

conversion: Which crystals should I buy for my meditation room?

trust: Is there science behind sacred space setup?

schema.howto_name: How to Set Up Crystals in a Meditation Room

hero.alt: A calm meditation corner with crystals placed at the corners of a low shelf and a central clear quartz, a folded cushion and a candle, soft natural light

step_diagrams.alt: Top-down view of a meditation space showing four corner stones and one central stone arranged on a cloth, a candle and singing bowl nearby
